text.title: Opportunity Solution Trees Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Opportunity Solution Trees methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Every OST MUST have exactly one metric-based outcome at the root (name + current value + target).
rule.rationale: Opportunityless trees collapse into unordered wishlists; one outcome focuses discovery.

rule.statement: Solutions MUST hang off an opportunity node; no orphan solutions.
rule.rationale: Solutions without opportunities are feature requests, not discovery.

rule.statement: Every solution node MUST spawn >=1 falsifiable assumption with a named test type (smoke/fake-door/prototype/Mom-Test).
rule.rationale: Without falsifiability, solution work proceeds without grounding.

rule.statement: Opportunities MUST carry numeric scores: frequency (n observations), severity (1-5), addressability (H/M/L); composite displayed with week-over-week delta.
rule.rationale: Gut-feel prioritization is unauditable; deltas reveal urgency shifts.

rule.statement: Tree depth MUST NOT exceed 4 levels (outcome -> sub-outcome -> opportunity -> solution).
rule.rationale: Deeper trees signal under-decomposed nodes that should split into separate trees.

rule.statement: Every monthly review MUST emit a kill list (parked/dead opportunities) >=1 entry after 4 weeks; without pruning OSTs collapse under their own weight.
rule.rationale: Opportunities accumulate faster than they resolve; pruning keeps the tree iterable.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec — decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
